title: ranunculus philonotis subsp. cordigerus
intro: Ranunculus philonotis subsp. cordigerus, also known as the 'Mountain Crowfoot', is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or pale yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. Consuming the plant or using its extracts is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus philonotis subsp. cordigerus is often employed in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, though its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
